meta.title: Alejandro Moreno Martín — Product Designer
meta.description: Product Designer specialising in digital interfaces, design systems, and user experience. Open to new opportunities.

hero.name: Alejandro Moreno
hero.tagline: Browse my work, read my story, or reach out.

about.label: About
about.heading: About
about.p1: I'm a multidisciplinary profile: I design interfaces, build design systems, write code, and think in terms of product. I don't fit neatly into one box — and I don't think I need to.
about.p2: What defines me most is a curiosity that never really switches off. I learn fast, adapt to very different environments, and find a path forward even when things get complicated.
about.p3: I work best when design and development coexist from the start. More intention in the decisions, less friction between teams, and better outcomes in the final product.

tools.title: Figma
tools.description: Core UI/UX design tool. Full cycle: wireframes, components, design systems and dev handoff.
tools.comment: I've used it for years and it's still the benchmark. Nothing else matches its iteration speed.

tools.title: FigJam
tools.description: Collaborative whiteboard from the Figma ecosystem. For discovery workshops, flow maps and team alignment.
tools.comment: It's where scattered thinking becomes structure before opening Figma.

tools.title: Astro
tools.description: Content-first web framework with static output by default. Fast, clean and TypeScript-native.
tools.comment: I rediscovered the pleasure of building websites when I first used it. No overhead, no magic.

tools.title: Tailwind CSS
tools.description: Utility-first CSS framework. Implements design tokens directly in markup with full consistency.
tools.comment: When you have a design system, Tailwind turns it into code without any middle layer.

tools.title: TypeScript
tools.description: Typed superset of JavaScript. Mandatory in all my projects — reduces errors and makes code maintainable.
tools.comment: Types aren't bureaucracy — they're living documentation the editor understands.

tools.title: React Native
tools.description: Native mobile applications with JavaScript. One codebase for iOS and Android with Expo.
tools.comment: The Expo ecosystem makes it accessible without sacrificing native experience.

tools.title: VS Code
tools.description: My primary editor. Extensible, fast and with native support for everything in my stack.
tools.comment: With the right extensions it stops being an editor and becomes an environment.

tools.title: Claude Code
tools.description: AI CLI integrated into the terminal and editor. Reasons through code, refactors and proposes solutions with full context.
tools.comment: It changed how I work. It's not autocomplete — it's a collaborator that understands context.

tools.title: GitHub
tools.description: Version control, code reviews and deployment pipelines integrated with every repository.
tools.comment: Every project starts with a repo. It's the thread connecting design, code and deployment.

tools.title: Vercel
tools.description: Deployment platform for web projects. GitHub integration, branch previews and built-in analytics.
tools.comment: Deploying shouldn't be work. With Vercel, it isn't.

tools.title: Notion
tools.description: For project documentation, research notes and client briefs. Flexible both solo and in a team.
tools.comment: The place where projects have context before they have code.

tools.title: Hotjar
tools.description: User behaviour analytics: heatmaps, session recordings and conversion funnels to understand exactly how visitors interact with a website.
tools.comment: Data tells you what's happening. Hotjar shows you why.

projects.title: Casa del Aire
projects.headline: A booking-focused site that cut direct enquiry time by 60%
projects.summary: Designed and developed the full website for a rural house in Extremadura, replacing a generic listing with a conversion-first experience owned by the client.

projects.title: Senzo Studio
projects.headline: A portfolio site that turned cold visits into qualified leads
projects.summary: Built the digital presence for a Madrid VFX agency from scratch, translating their visual language into a client-facing site that showcases work and drives contact.

projects.title: Forma
projects.headline: A progress-tracking app designed for clarity over complexity
projects.summary: Designed a mobile app for users who want precise, visual control of their body composition — without the noise of generic fitness platforms.

projects.title: Sazón
projects.headline: A recipe app that makes cooking feel personal again
projects.summary: Designed a social recipe book where users log, organise, and share their favourite dishes — turning a private habit into a community experience.

items.description: Since 2021 I've been running design and development projects end to end, typically with 8 to 10 clients a year across very different sectors. The work spans design systems, interfaces, and fully functional websites. On projects with a strong conversion focus, optimised funnels have reduced CPL by 10 to 15%, growing leads by up to 18%.

I co-founded a local B2C business model that grew to serve over 200 active customers. I handled operations, marketing, sales, and logistics simultaneously while testing the MVP and refining the product based on direct user feedback.

Three years leading the commercial side of a B2B industrial company with €5M in annual revenue. I designed a strategy that grew the sales pipeline by €1.2M — combining public tenders, key account management, and the gradual digitalisation of the commercial channel.

I managed digital reputation and online communities for the agency. In a short time, active users grew by 12%, the community gained 3,000 new followers, and negative mentions dropped by 20% through a structured crisis management approach.

items.title: Bachelor's Degree in Marketing
items.description: Training in commercial strategy, consumer behaviour, communication, and digital marketing.

items.title: UI Pattern Design for Successful Software
Emotional Design: How to Create Products That Are Loved
Design for the 21st Century with Don Norman
User Research: Methods and Good Practices

contact.heading: Let's talk
contact.description: I'm looking to join a team where design has real impact. If you have an interesting project or an opportunity that fits, reach out.
contact.availableFor: Available for: full-time role · freelance · collaborations
